title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its 10th radar-imaging satellite for Synspective, a Japanese company, on June 26. The mission was delayed due to a previous responsive space mission.

title: Spain-backed fund joins FOSSA’s sovereign satellite communications push
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems raised $10.5 million to expand its satellite connectivity constellation, with funding from a Spain-backed investment vehicle. The round supports FOSSA's push for sovereign satellite communications.

title: Starlink veterans launch startup to broaden megaconstellation ownership
summary: Former SpaceX engineers who worked on Starlink have founded a new startup to provide megaconstellation services to governments and companies. The venture aims to offer more control over space-based infrastructure.

title: ISS repair spacewalk highlights concerns about station health
summary: NASA astronauts are preparing for a spacewalk to repair the International Space Station's robotic arm, while advisers express concerns about the station's long-term health and spacesuit reliability.

title: Europe’s next security challenge is in orbit
summary: Europe faces a growing security challenge in space, with satellite navigation underpinning over 10% of its GDP. The reliance on space-based systems highlights the need for enhanced protection of orbital assets.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions on three Electron rockets in 2027. This contract award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream has released the first light imagery from its GEMS2-Amethyst satellite's microwave radiometer, marking a milestone in global atmospheric observations. The company, based in Boulder, Colorado, is advancing commercial weather satellite capabilities.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military missions.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining a growing list of countries committed to cooperative space exploration practices. This move aligns Botswana with international efforts to promote peaceful and sustainable space activities.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions, which have become a bottleneck. The demand for SpaceX's Falcon 9 Transporter and Bandwagon rideshare services has led to limited availability for smaller payloads.

title: Blue Origin plans to fly New Glenn rocket again this year despite massive rocket explosion (video)
summary: Blue Origin aims to fly its New Glenn rocket again this year after a massive explosion, with CEO Dave Limp setting an ambitious goal to complete repairs and relaunch before the end of 2026.

title: NASA used a drone to deliver a human kidney. Is this the future of transplant transport?
summary: NASA tested a drone to deliver a human kidney beyond line of sight, exploring future transplant transport possibilities. The kidney was not viable for transplant but served as a concept test.

title: Strange glowing 'bow-and-arrow' structure may be a giant cosmic shock wave created by a supersonic galaxy collision
summary: A massive 'bow-and-arrow' radio galaxy, spanning 1.8 million light-years, may be a giant cosmic shock wave resulting from a supersonic galaxy collision. This structure could provide insights into how galaxy clusters shape the universe's largest structures.

title: Black hole's 'point of no escape' studied with the loudest gravitational waves ever heard
summary: Scientists detected the loudest gravitational waves ever recorded, originating from a black hole merger. This discovery offers a new method to study event horizons, the boundaries of black holes from which nothing can escape.

title: 'Exodus: The Helium Sea' author Peter F. Hamilton talks universe crafting and finishing the story in this second prequel novel for sci-fi RPG 'Exodus' (exclusive)
summary: Author Peter F. Hamilton discusses universe crafting and completing the story in his second prequel novel for the sci-fi RPG 'Exodus'. The novel explores the concept of sailing ships in space.

title: SpaceX's next Starship breathes fire for 1st time in prelaunch test (video)
summary: SpaceX conducted a prelaunch test firing of its next Starship rocket, marking progress in preparations for its next launch. This follows the first launch of the 'Version 3' Starship just a month prior.

title: China plans to double the size of its Tiangong space station while the ISS nears its end
summary: China intends to expand its Tiangong space station by adding three new modules and a co-orbiting telescope, while the International Space Station (ISS) approaches its planned deorbiting and splashdown in the Pacific Ocean.

title: Only a few hours left! This expert-approved, beginner-friendly drone is $60 off for Prime Day
summary: A beginner-friendly drone, the DJI Neo, is being offered at a discounted price of $139 during Prime Day. Drone expert James Abbott recommends it for newcomers.

title: Moore's law tells us that the price of technology should go down over time, so why is stargazing gear getting more expensive?
summary: Stargazing gear prices are rising despite Moore's Law, with high-end smart telescopes and binoculars costing thousands due to various market factors.

title: Beam me up to 4K: Your ultimate sci-fi collection for less than $25 on Prime Day
summary: Amazon is offering the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus at a significant discount during Prime Day, making it accessible for less than $25. This deal is a limited-time offer ending today.

title: NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge
summary: NASA revealed the winners of the 2026 Human Lander Challenge, honoring student-developed solutions for life support systems in lunar landers. The initiative supports the Artemis program, aiming to return astronauts to the Moon.

title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
summary: NASA is testing a new refueling device for future in-space missions, enabling spacecraft to refuel in Earth orbit before deep space exploration. The technology resembles a gas pump nozzle for spacecraft fuel tanks.

title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
summary: NASA and partners are preparing for the June 30 launch of the Swift Boost Mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory. This mission aims to extend the operational life of the observatory.

title: NASA Identifies More Than 40 Space Technologies for Collaboration
summary: NASA selected 41 proposals from 37 companies to advance space technologies supporting long-term lunar presence and Mars exploration. The selected American companies will work on technologies for space transportation, planetary surface operations, and lunar sustainability.

title: Euclid Sees Heart of Milky Way
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, captured a new image of the heart of the Milky Way galaxy. The observation aligns with a region that NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study later this summer.

title: Bringing Signals to NASA
summary: Eric Fernandez's childhood fascination with rockets led him to a career in the space industry, inspired by watching launches with his father and his grandparents' service at Vandenberg Air Force Base.

title: NASA’s PACE Mission Studies Smoke, Fires
summary: NASA's PACE satellite is monitoring vegetation precursors to fires, smoke plumes, and their movement during the North American fire season. The data will help scientists better understand wildfires.

title: Hubble Spies Starry Chandelier
summary: The NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster NGC 6723, also known as the Chandelier Cluster. This starry formation is located in the constellation Sagittarius.

title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
summary: Astronauts and cosmonauts from Expedition 73 shared mission experiences and honored ground teams at Space Center Houston. The event celebrated the completion of NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and Crew-11 missions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Additionally, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about its 25 missions.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the upcoming launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning. The test was launched aboard a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew at the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 robotic arm as the CRS-34 cargo mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift vehicle after transporting its second stage to the Stennis Space Center. The company is making significant progress in the development of the Terran R.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten space launches are scheduled across multiple global sites in the upcoming week. This marks a busy period for the space industry with frequent orbital missions.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace aborted the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider had been preparing for weeks before the scrub.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. SpaceX's Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. The technology aims to streamline operations and reduce costs in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its main instrument, ASPIICS, are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with their expertise.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a technological advancement or structural innovation in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: Former SpaceX employees have collectively raised $9.2 billion for their new ventures, highlighting SpaceX's role as a talent incubator. The data underscores the company's impact on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in the space industry.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: A New Crop of Early Stage Space Startups Emerges
summary: A new generation of early-stage space startups has emerged, marking a growing trend in the space industry. These startups are highlighted in a recent article from Payload Pro.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to meet growing demand. This expansion aims to support emerging players in the space industry.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission focused on mitigating hacking threats in orbit and shared-compute modules on rideshare satellites. Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution, aims to address these growing cybersecurity challenges.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO, this month. The network aims to enhance domestic satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association to represent non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This new trade association aims to advocate for the interests of NGSO operators in the evolving space industry.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the reentry sector. This event validates competition and innovation among startups in space reentry technology.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. This is highlighted in a report from the space agency's inspector general.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted the readiness of commercial markets for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, but noted a persistent gap in understanding how to effectively communicate these solutions to customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications, a Canadian satellite communications provider, for up to CAD $52 million ($36.6 million). The deal aims to expand Calian's satellite connectivity and remote solutions offerings.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. FlexMaritime combines SES’s satellite network with integrated solutions to enhance maritime connectivity.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured 4.6 million euro ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its 'OrbitCloud' R&D program, aimed at developing a space-based edge computing network. The initiative is supported by Business Finland.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus received a $5 million award from NASA to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The technology will support NASA's future lunar and deep-space missions.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion and Voyager Technologies are collaborating with other entities to advance U.S. quantum space initiatives. The partnership includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA’s Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth’s atmosphere. The mission aims to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation has contracted MDA Space to develop a digital payload for a next-generation Japanese defense communications satellite. The contract includes the construction of digital payloads and antennas.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its newest SAR satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first synthetic aperture radar images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct the buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to become operational by 2029.
